templates.name: Do you offer expedited shipping?/Overnight shipping
templates.content: I'm afraid, we don't offer overnight shipping at the moment. If you would like expedited shipping, we have 2-day and next-day shipping options available at additional rates. Please note that expedited deliveries do not deliver on weekends and holidays. You can check this link for more info: https://solostove.attn.tv/a3QYOHLLdnGy

templates.name: Do your surround bundles include the new removable base plate and ash pan?
templates.content: All of the surround bundles include a 2.0 unit that has a removable ash pan and grate.

templates.name: Does this Yukon + Surround Bundle include the Yukon 1.0 or the 2.0?
templates.content: The Yukon + Surround Bundles comes with the Yukon 2.0 version.

templates.name: What is the difference between Yukon backyard bundle 1.0 and 2.0?
templates.content: The only difference between 2.0 and 1.0 is the removable ash pan. This allows you to enjoy the same smokeless flame but with an easier ash removal process.

templates.name: Will you be including the receipt in the package?
templates.content: No, there won't be a billing invoice with the prices included in the box that gets shipped out. This invoice/receipt is only emailed to you, the customer.

templates.name: What are the ingredients for each product in the Artisan kit?
templates.content:
The Neapolitan dough is made from "00" Caputo flour, water, salt, fresh yeast, and evaporated cane juice. The San Marzano tomato pizza sauce is made from San Marzano Italian tomatoes, onions, olive oil, salt, black pepper, basil, oregano, and garlic.

The Losurdo fresh mozzarella is made from pasteurized milk, vinegar, salt, and enzymes and the Ezzo pepperoni contains pork, beef, salt, spices, sugar, water, extractive of paprika, natural smoke flavor, lactic acid starter culture, garlic powder, sodium nitrite, ascorbic acid, BHA, BHT, and citric acid.

templates.name: How far apart do your pits need to be from each other if burning 2 or more?
templates.content: When you double up on the fun, take care to place each fire pit in a safe place according to our preferred safety recommendations. Your Solo Stoves can be any distance apart, as long as they are not touching each other. You'll need safe access to each Solo later on in the burn for roasting marshmallows and stoking the flames, so plan accordingly!

templates.name: What's the difference between Bonfire 1.0 and Bonfire 2.0?
templates.content: Both versions come with a detachable Grate and Ashpan trail but on the 2.0 version, you can take it on and off so you can clean it better. On the 1.0 version it's attached to the firepit so you can't remove it and the only way for cleaning it is to flip around the firepit to dump the ashes.

templates.name: Do you offer price adjustments/can you retroactively add the free gift to my order?
templates.content:
The best way to handle this is to contact us via this link: https://solostove.com/en-us/contact or call/text us at 817-900-2664. Our team will be more than happy to help out!

Please note that our team is available Monday - Friday, 8am-8pm and Saturday- Sunday
8am-5pm CST.

templates.name: Social media
templates.content: Give us a follow on IG: https://www.instagram.com/solostove/ and FB here: https://www.facebook.com/solostove/

templates.name: Restock - Solo Stove (autosend)
templates.content: I currently don't have the exact date of a possible restock, but I recommend checking our website and social media frequently. Is there anything else you like?

templates.name: Order Upsell
templates.content: By the way, I think these fire starter packs would go really well with your order: https://www.solostove.com/fire-starters/

templates.name: Using propane on the PI, how long does it take to reach the right temperature?
templates.content: There are two different temperatures to know - stone temp and ambient air temp. You may see some variability in these numbers as you use Pi: Wood Burning stone temp is 750°F, and ambient air is 850°F. Gas Burner stone is 800°F and ambient air is 900°F. The wood-burning setup will vary depending on wood type, the time it takes to get hot, the quantity of fuel added, and the frequency of adding the wood. Outside ambient conditions can also affect stone and air temperatures inside the oven.

templates.name: Is the shelter compatible with the shield and deflector?
templates.content: The shield can be flipped upside down to tuck inside the pit to use with the shelter. Since the deflector is larger than the pit it can not be stored under the shelter.

templates.name: What grade steel do you use?
templates.content: We use 18/8 304 stainless steel on everything except the grate, which is made of 430 stainless. Because of the high heat of the grate all grades of stainless perform pretty similar. The 304 and 18/8 are the same thing. 430 is also known as 18/0. 18/10 is also a 300 series stainless but has a little higher nickel percentage and more commonly used for cookware and flatware. 18/8 is the grade of US standardized 304.

templates.name: Is the Campfire compatible with the alcohol burner?
templates.content: The Campfire isn't compatible with the alcohol burner.

templates.name: Can you ship at a later date/delay shipping?
templates.content: We don't currently have a way to delay shipping. So sorry about that!

templates.name: Store location / retailer
templates.content: You can find us at a store near you here: https://www.solostove.com/dealer-locator/

templates.name: Where are your items made/manufactured?
templates.content: Our products are designed in Texas and shipped from one of our three fulfillment centers in TX, Pennsylvania or Utah. As far as manufacturing, we have manufacturers in several countries including China, Vietnam and India.

templates.name: Where do you ship from?
templates.content: Our products are designed in Texas and shipped from one of our three fulfillment centers in TX, Pennsylvania or Utah.

templates.name: Why am I charged sales tax? I live in a tax-free state
templates.content: There's a brief connection failure between our system and an automatic tax provider. The disconnect forces the system to use a default 10% or manual tax rate application and overcharge. Usually, this reverts to the correct amount when you get to the final stage at check out. If for some reason this does not happen for you, let me know and my team will get it straightened out. Please note that tax is also charged by the 'ship to state' and not the state in which you reside.

templates.name: Can the fire pits be used in an enclosed space/porch with no roof damage?
templates.content: You'll need to keep 20 to 25 feet of open space above your fire pit. These flames can be four feet tall, and the flame ring guides those flames straight up ahead. Having anything above your fire pit might not be the safest idea!

templates.name: Can I finance using Affirm?
templates.content: Unfortunately, we no longer offer Affirm as a payment method. Klarna is the only financing option we offer at the moment.

templates.name: Return policy
templates.content: You can check out our detailed return policy here: https://www.solostove.com/shipping-returns/

templates.name: Dimensions of the shipping box for the Yukon Shelter
templates.content: The size of the box is 14 x 6 x 3.

templates.name: Customer support
templates.content: The best way to handle this is to email us directly at help@solostove.com or give us a call at 817-900-2664. Our team is happy to help!

templates.name: Can I use pre-logs or artificial logs?
templates.content: You can absolutely burn Duraflame logs, or other compressed brick logs in your fire pit! They're a great way to get a raging fire started. That being said, there will be more smoke with these logs. Only thing to note - don't use charcoal or any kind of accelerant in your fire pit! This can cause the stainless steel to have a bad reaction. Also, using an accelerant might just mean your fire pit will wear out sooner than later. We recommend dry hard woods!

templates.name: Is the package slip discreet?
templates.content: The product box displays a picture and the name of the product.

templates.name: Do you offer gift wrapping?
templates.content: I'm afraid we don't offer gift wrapping. We suggest shipping it to an alternative location if you're planning a surprise!

templates.name: Do you price match with the 3rd party retailers?
templates.content: Unfortunately, we don't price match with any third party retailers.

templates.name: Cancel / edit order
templates.content: Since our team is so fast at fulfilling orders, sometimes we can't catch the orders before they're shipped out. Let's just say it's similar to finding a needle in a haystack, we can't remove the order once it has been shipped. You can submit a return label through our Return portal here - https://returns.narvar.com/solostove/returns?locale=en_US I apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

templates.name: Shipping protection / What is Route Insurance?
templates.content: Route Insurance allows you to buy additional shipping insurance for all orders, covering loss, damage, or theft while your item is en route. They take the hassle out of the equation, and handle everything from replacement costs to shipping labels when things go wrong. All you have to do is submit a quick claim, and they'll get to work for you. While we would provide the same guarantee for shipping matters, our expertise is in crafting shareable moments and Route has us beat when it comes to simplifying lengthy processes.

templates.name: When do I see my points reflected on my account?
templates.content: Your points will be added once your entire order has shipped.

templates.name: How close can my firepit be to cement blocks around it?
templates.content: You can put your fire pit in an enclosure, just make sure there are 3-6'' of space around your fire pit so you don't block off any airflow and you should be good to go!

templates.name: Why does patina happen?/ How to clean the patina?
templates.content: In the presence of oxygen, all stainless steels will slowly form a chromium oxide "scale" layer on exposed surfaces. We see this as a patina or discoloration in the steel, which increases over time. For those who prefer, the patina can be cleaned using Bar Keeper's Friend and for others the patina becomes a badge of honor, changing over time with each new memory.

templates.name: Can I use it on a wood deck/composite surface?
templates.content: When paired with the Stand, you can use our fire pits on a wooden deck safely. Composite decking, such as Trex, TimberTech, Azek, TIVA, Nexan, and Versadeck, have a manufacturer fire resistance rating from A to C. You can check with your manufacturer or builder for what grade decking you have beforehand. To be safe, using a stand is required at all times when burning on composite and PVC decking. The very bottom rim of your Solo Stove is actually the coolest part of the unit, so a little insulation between your Solo and the surface it will rest on goes a long way. You can find more information on that here : https://blog.solostove.com/when-to-use-a-fire-pit-stand/ We also offer a spark arresting shield for extra protection against popping embers that might find their way in places they don't belong.

templates.name: The item I ordered a while ago from your website is cheaper now! Can you adjust the price?
templates.content: If a price changes within 30 days of your initial purchase, we're able to price match!

templates.name: Do you have catalogs that show prices?
templates.content: At this time we don't have any catalogs that offer pricing.

templates.name: If I don't get route insurance, will you still guarantee my purchase if it gets damaged or lost in transit?
templates.content: Route is simply a faster way to get your replacement and you're not required to return the stove, so it takes a lot of the burden off you. We will of course get you taken care of if your fire-pit is damaged, it'll just take a little while longer and you'll have to go through the return process.

templates.name: Dimensions, weight, heat range and usage suggestions for Solo Stoves
templates.content: Our fire pits come in 3 different sizes so you can take on any environment. Think of them as Small, Medium, and Large, but instead it's Ranger, Bonfire, and Yukon. The Ranger is 15 pounds, and 15'' in diameter. It's perfect for 1-4 people and the typical heat radius is about 3-4 feet around. The Bonfire weighs 20 pounds, and is 19.5'' in diameter. It's great for 3-8 people and its typical heat radius is about 5-6 feet around. The Yukon weighs 38 pounds and is 27'' in diameter. It's great for 6-10 people, and the heat radius is about 6-7 feet around.  If you want more information, this blog is an awesome resource that should be able to answer questions you might not have realized you had: https://solostove.attn.tv/sl/jldftVOG

templates.name: Why can't I use Klarna at checkout? (for orders over $1,000)
templates.content: Unfortunately, Klarna only accepts orders up to $1,000. Anything over that won't be eligible for financing via them. I apologize for the inconvenience.

templates.name: Can I add protection plans on bundles?
templates.content: Currently the protection plans are only available for the stand alone pits and aren't offered for the bundles, however, our bundles will be covered under our performance warranty!

templates.name: Can you use the Solo Stove in an enclosed room?
templates.content: We don't recommend placing it under any enclosure due to the small amount of carbon dioxide emitted during the fire.

templates.name: Can I use propane with your stoves / fire pits?
templates.content: We don't recommend using any accelerants in your fire pits as this can cause the stainless steel to have a bad reaction! Also, using an accelerant might just mean your fire pit will wear out sooner than later. We recommend using natural fuel sources, like dryer lint, pine cones, leaves, and even our Starters aren't bad either. Keeping it natural will preserve your fire pit for years and years to come.

templates.name: With a stand, what is the temperature rating underneath the fire pit? Will the stand keep it cool?
templates.content: The stand keeps the pit about 70 degrees underneath.

templates.name: Dimensions of the shipping box for the Bonfire
templates.content: The size of the box is 22x22x17.

templates.name: Can you use soy wax fire starters with your stoves/fire pits?
templates.content: Yes! Those are great fire starters.

templates.name: Can I use different pans on the Campfire? Where do I place them?
templates.content: You can use various pans on the campfire. They sit on top of the cooking ring that comes with the pit.

templates.name: Sold Out Product Rec [INSERT LINK]
templates.content: Sorry that product is sold out at the moment. What do you think about this instead [link]?

templates.name: Can you use the heat deflector at the same time as the grill?
templates.content: The heat deflector wasn't designed to be used at the same time as the grill.

templates.name: Can you take phone orders?
templates.content: We can! Just give us a call at 817-900-2664 from 8am-12am CST.

templates.name: Can I use the stove on (whatever surface the customer has)?
templates.content: You can use your fire pit on any surface, as long as you use a Stand. Our fire pits have a raised ash pan so the embers are never directly resting on the surface below. That being said, a Stand is required on heat sensitive surfaces, such as wood decks. If you plan on using it on a deck made of composite materials like Trex, Azek, Fiberon, Timbertech, etc., they have a manufacturer fire resistance rating from A to C. Check with your manufacturer or builder for what grade decking you have beforehand. To be safe, using a stand is required at all times when burning on composite and PVC decking. If you're curious about when to use a Stand, check out our blog with all the answers - https://solostove.attn.tv/sl/tf4vfxCL

templates.name: Phone support
templates.content: You can give us a call at 817-900-2664 or let me know if I can help via text, it may be faster.

templates.name: Can I mix and match or add/remove items from the bundle deals?
templates.content: I'm afraid the bundles are pre-packaged and priced, so we're unable to mix and match.

templates.name: Just Looking Best Sellers
templates.content:
Sounds good. Here are our most popular products at the moment: https://solostove.com/en-us/c/fire-pits
Does anything catch your eye?

templates.name: Can the ranger with the wok be used as a fire pit as well?
templates.content: You can definitely remove the wok anytime you want to use it as a fire pit!

templates.name: Temperature range of your fire pits
templates.content: The temperature range depends on the size of the fire you have going. The bottom of the pit can reach temperatures over 150 degrees. Please note that it wouldn't be safe to use the fire pit without a stand or pavers.

templates.name: Can I add a gift note / gift message to my order?
templates.content: We offer gift notes/cards through this link: https://email.solostove.com/gift-messaging-card/ All you need to do is print off the card or download the card and send it to your friend or family! I'm afraid at this time we don't have the ability to add these to orders.

templates.name: Do you offer split payments
templates.content: Unfortunately, you can't split payments at checkout, but there's a little trade secret I can share with you to solve this problem. Instead of splitting it, you can purchase a gift card, and it'll be emailed to you. You can then use that balance towards your purchase, and use the other form of payment you had in mind. This is the easiest fix!

templates.name: Wholesale inquiries
templates.content: Thank you for your interest! The best way to discuss wholesale is to email us directly at  help@solostove.com. Our team is happy to chat.

templates.name: Warranty policy
templates.content: Our lifetime warranty covers any defective product purchased from us or an authorized retailer. Issues arising from normal wear or misuse won't be covered by warranty. You can take a look at our full warranty policy here: https://solo-stove-help-center.gorgias.help/en-US/lifetime-warranty-107767 And you can start a claim here: https://www.solostove.com/warranty-claims/

templates.name: How many pieces of wood comes in the Juniper Firewood bundle?
templates.content: Each Juniper firewood bundle is a convenient power pack of 6-8 logs equalling 0.5 cubic feet of firewood.

templates.name: Where do I find my referral code?
templates.content: First, open your Firestarter Rewards window and sign in to your account. Go to Referrals and there you should have your shareable code.

templates.name: What is the lid used for? / Can I use the lid to put out the fire?
templates.content: The lid's main function is to create a tabletop with your fire pit when not in use, or as a temporary cover to keep out rain and dust when your fire is completely out. Be advised that it's never recommended to use it to snuff the fire.

templates.name: Rewards / referral program
templates.content: We have a rewards/referral program where you can earn points to redeem on future purchases. Check it out here - https://www.solostove.com/en-us/firestarter-rewards

templates.name: What's the difference between Mesa and Mesa XL?
templates.content: Mesa XL has a pellet capacity of about 3x more than Mesa, and will subsequently burn for about 15 minutes longer (45-60min total with pellets). It will also emit more heat while burning, due to more fuel being consumed. Both Mesa and Mesa XL are very easy to light, will have about the same flame height when burning, and are extremely portable and functional for your outdoor spaces!

templates.name: Military/First Responder/Medical/Teacher discount
templates.content: Thank you for your service. You can get your hero discount here: https://www.solostove.com/hero-discount/?utm_campaign=concierge&utm_medium=text&utm_source=attentive&externalId=mX4ZW It's for $15 off.

templates.name: Can I use wood pellets in a Solo Stove fire pit?
templates.content:
We don't suggest using wood pellets in a Solo Stove fire pit because they can fall through the bottom grate and get stuck inside. If the wood pellets get wet or come in contact with moisture, they can swell and cause damage to the unit. 

I'd recommend taking a look at our Fire Pit Pellet Adapter which will optimize your fire pit for a pellet-fueled burn and turn it into the ultimate dual-fuel backyard: https://solostove.com/en-us/p/fire-pit-pellet-adapter?option_id=1279&queryID=ca8e62c1fc2a0cbdfbfcb7d783719b88centerpiece.

templates.name: Will the Solo Stove rust if left outside?
templates.content: We use 304 stainless steel, which has a resistance to rust, but it's not totally rust-proof. Prolonged exposure to harsh weather will increase rust & discoloration. I would recommend always storing it in a cool, dry location. If you've got a Shelter, our 100% weatherproof cover, use it as often as possible.

templates.name: Are your products at Walmart?
templates.content: Walmart is an Authorized dealer - all our branded items sold there, per our agreement, are authentic.

templates.name: Would solo stove  leave a mark without a stand?
templates.content: Under normal recommended use (3-4 hours), our fire pits won't get hot enough to break down concrete, however, if you're burning the fire pit on painted, stained, or stamped concrete, you should use a Stand to ensure no damage is done to the concrete finish.

templates.name: Shipping time
templates.content: Our processing time takes 2 business days and we usually ship via UPS or FedEx. Once shipped it will take 3-5 business days for domestic orders.

templates.name: Brand ambassador / sponsorship / affiliate program
templates.content: Thank you for your interest! You can learn more and sign up for our influencer program here - https://solostove.grin.live/ If you have more questions on partnerships, please email us at marketing@solostove.com. Our team is happy to chat.

templates.name: How do I redeem/claim rewards?
templates.content: Please log into your account, and then click on "Rewards" (the rosette sign on the top right corner next to the cart). Click on 'Ways to Redeem' in the popup, and then click 'Redeem'. Let me know how it goes!

templates.name: Do you ship to APO/FPO/DPO addresses?
templates.content: First off, we would like to thank you for your sacrifice and service. While we are unable to ship to APO/FPO/DPO addresses, we partner with govx.com to support our overseas military bases and encourage you to shop for our products through them. Again, we want to thank you for all you've done and continue to do.

templates.name: Is the [item] in stock in [store]?
templates.content: You can look up your local dealers here: https://solostove.attn.tv/sl/z7kFtzKh and call them directly to see if they have it in stock.

templates.name: Does the Ranger / Bonfire come with the carry case?
templates.content: Yes, all Ranger & Bonfire Fire Pits and Bundle Fire Pit options come with carry cases included in the purchase.

templates.name: What's the difference between Lite, Titan, and Campfire Camp Stoves?
templates.content: Aside from weight and portability, the main difference is size. The Solo Stove Lite is good for 1-2 people, the Titan is good for 2-4 people and the larger Campfire works great for groups of 4+ people.

templates.name: What are the differences between your bundles?
templates.content: The Essentials Bundle includes accessories needed for the job - a lid, carry case, stand, etc. The Backyard Bundle includes more accessories like a shelter. The Ultimate Bundle includes every accessory you could need to create the most memorable experience possible, like roasting sticks, tools, etc. Finally, the Cooking Bundle includes accessories needed for cooking like an iron griddle or a wok, etc. Hope it helps.

templates.name: How do I clean Heat Deflector and how often should I clean it?
templates.content: With preventative care, like keeping the Heat Deflector out of the rain and humidity, cleaning sessions should be few and far between. After a few uses, some soot will build up on the bottom of the Heat Deflector. We recommend polishing Heat Deflector's surfaces with Barkeepers Friend and a dry cloth. Always ensure Heat Deflector is completely dry before storing to prevent surface rust.

templates.name: How far do the fire pits need to be from LP cylinders?
templates.content:
Our fire pits can produce flames up to 4 feet high and are hotter than the average ground fire. We always recommend keeping at least 30 feet between your fire pit and any highly flammable or explosive substances - like LP cylinders. 

We've found this recommendation to be the safest way to enjoy your fire pit, especially so close to home. It's always a great idea to double check with your local fire department about their preferred safety regulations.

templates.name: Can I use my fire pit in an existing fire pit?
templates.content: Placing your Solo Stove in your existing fire pit, tailor-made DIY surround, or below-ground pit is a great way to make the backyard fire an inviting hearth. Just 3 or more inches of space around the exterior wall of the Solo Stove and any ring or surround will give plenty of airflow to your fire so you can keep creating good moments.

templates.name: Can I use wood in my Grill?
templates.content: Grill works hard to bring your recipes to life with a fuel that works for you. Your favorite firewood, lump coal, charcoal, or chunk wood can help you craft the perfect flame in your Grill. Our heat-up times are based on charcoal, and will naturally vary with the use of other fuels. Smoking woods on top of charcoal is a great way to enhance the flavor of foods.

templates.name: What is the cool down time for each fire pit and camp stove?
templates.content: Depending on how intense your fire was burning, and the ambient temperature outside, it may be 1-3 hours before your Solo Stove is cool enough to handle. Some ways to speed up the cooling process are smothering the coals with sand or ash, or using heat-proof gloves to discard the ashes.

templates.name: What does “fire resistant” mean?
templates.content: Flame-resistant or fire-resistant materials are designed to prevent the spread of fire and withstand heat. Fire-resistant materials should self-extinguish once the source of ignition is removed. Keep in mind that after a certain amount of time and heat level, anything will burn eventually. Please note that stray embers could burn holes through the fire resistant material.

templates.name: Does Pi go on my fire pit?
templates.content: Pi is a stand-alone tool for improving your good moments. This wood-firing pizza oven works independently from Solo Stove fire pits.

templates.name: How much Mini Oak Firewood do I get with my purchase?
templates.content: Each package comes with approximately 20lbs of Mini Oak Firewood.

templates.name: When will I receive my refund?
templates.content: Please allow one week of processing after the package has arrived at our facility. Once it has been allocated into our system, we will issue store credit or a refund back to the original payment method.

templates.name: How long can I keep the frozen ingredients from the Artisan Pizza Box in the freezer?
templates.content: The Neapolitan dough is best thawed and used within 4 months of delivery. The San Marzano tomato pizza sauce is best thawed and used within 16 months of delivery. The Losurdo fresh mozzarella is best thawed and used within 5 months of delivery and the Ezzo pepperoni is best thawed and used within 10 months of delivery.

templates.name: Can I return my product that was purchased at a store?
templates.content: Any products that are unopened and in their original packaging that were purchased from a third party can be returned and given store credit for the current sale price if a different size is requested.

templates.name: Is this blanket waterproof?
templates.content: All NanoLoft Flame blankets are treated with 90/10 DWR and are highly water-resistant, but aren't waterproof.

templates.name: How much Mini Oak Firewood do I need for my Pi?
templates.content: We recommend starting with 4-5 pieces, then adding in two at a time to maintain your flame.

templates.name: How long will thawed Neapolitan Dough last at room temperature?
templates.content: We recommend using the dough within 6 hours of removing it from the fridge. The dough will not go bad, but the yeast will start to deteriorate.

templates.name: How much wood is included in the Oak bundle?
templates.content: Each Oak firewood bundle is a convenient power pack of 6-8 logs equalling 0.5 cubic feet of firewood.

templates.name: Can I use charcoal in my fire pit?
templates.content: We don't recommend burning charcoal in your fire pit as charcoal burns differently than wood. Our fire pits are designed to breathe and enhance the experience with wood. Charcoal fills the fire pit and suffocates it. It also burns much hotter than wood which could damage the pit and/or damage surfaces underneath the pit even when a stand is used.

templates.name: What is the difference between the Tall and Short stand for the Grill?
templates.content: The short stand is designed to enjoy a sharable grilling experience, to bring the kitchen experience outdoors, and to enjoy making a meal together. It's all about bringing the grill master from the isolated role back to the group experience. Since that is the ideal experience, the short stand comes with the bundle. We decided to make the tall stand optional for people who want a more traditional experience.

templates.name: Can Mesa be used under a canopy, patio umbrella, gazebo, etc?
templates.content: Mesa is designed for outdoor use only and is not recommended to use under a canopy, patio umbrella, or gazebo.

templates.name: How do I clean the Cast Iron Cooktop?
templates.content:
Grease and crumbs can be wiped away while the iron is hot, and you can do this safely with rags gripped by cooking tongs. Remove your cast iron from the Hub using heat-resistant gloves and rinse with just hot water. The most stubborn residue can be removed with our Cast Iron Scrubber and hot water. 

Follow all cleansing with thorough drying. Never let the cast iron soak or be submerged in water for any amount of time. Apply a light coat of seasoning oil to clean and dry cast iron before storing. Season your Cast Iron Cooktop well and often to keep it non-stick.

templates.name: Can the fire pit be used on the Grill stand?
templates.content: We do not recommend using the Fire Pit on the Grill Stand for safety reasons. Our Stands are tailor made to bring out the best in each Solo Stove. The greatest way to get your neighborhood marshmallow roast started smoothly, and safely, is to use the corresponding Stand for your Solo Stove.

templates.name: Are there preservatives in the Neapolitan Dough to increase shelf life?
templates.content: No, there aren't. Our Neapolitan dough is flash-frozen, allowing a long shelf life without the help of preservatives.

templates.name: How do I test the temperature to see if it is safe to touch the stove?
templates.content: You can use an IR thermometer to test the temperature or use heat-resistant gloves when in doubt.

templates.name: Is this blanket fireproof?
templates.content: NanoLoft Flame blankets are certified fire-resistant on the face side of the blanket but are not fireproof. Keep in mind, the back side is a ripstop polyester that is not fire-resistant.

templates.name: Will the color fade due to UV exposure?
templates.content: The ceramic coating is rated at 1000hrs of UV resistance. Therefore, yes, UV rays can eventually cause the color to begin to fade. We still recommend using a shelter to protect your fire pit when not in use. This will also help to protect the color from the sun.

templates.name: What is the typical heat radius of the fire pit?
templates.content: The heat output of your fire pit can naturally vary by your weather, firewood, sensitivity, and more. Generally, you can count on Ranger to keep a 3-4 foot heat radius, which makes it the perfect size for an intimate gathering. Bonfire's 5-6 foot heat radius has great versatility between backyard burning for a crowd or bringing a warm gathering place to your favorite destination. Yukon has the honor of being our largest fire pit, ideal for warming friends and family for every backyard celebration--big or small! The largest fire pit will naturally have a generous 6-7 foot heat radius.

templates.name: Will the stove damage the grass?
templates.content:
Using the fire pit with the Stand lifts the fire pit 2.75" off the ground and the Stand minimizes heat transfer to the surface below. If the fire pit has been left for an extended amount of time with the grass not being exposed to sunlight, it will cause the grass to die/discolor in that area. We recommend using a Stand when placing your fire pit on grass to provide enough space around the fire pit's lower vent holes and to protect the health of your lawn.

If your grass is tall and/or thick, it is possible your grass will still die even if using the Stand with your Solo Stove.

templates.name: Where can you use Pi?
templates.content: Any outdoor spaces you love to gather in will be improved by Pi. You can place Pi on a sturdy, waist-level surface that can handle a little heat. Granite, brick, masonry, tile, and unfinished wood are all good options.

templates.name: Can you use starters that aren’t Solo Stove starters?
templates.content: The beauty of Solo Stove is that our fire pits and camp stoves are made to light with all kinds of fuels and starters. Homemade dryer lint starters, scout-inspired found fuel starters, or other starters can be used. We offer various starters as well for customers that are looking for the perfect starter to use with our stoves.

templates.name: How warm is this blanket?
templates.content: While these blankets aren't temperature rated, they should keep you plenty warm in 45° weather and above.

templates.name: How do I store the Mini Oak Firewood?
templates.content: Mini Oak Firewood should be stored in a cool, dry place where it will not be exposed to moisture.

templates.name: What are the dimensions of the blanket?
templates.content: The Blanket's dimensions when laid flat are 38" x 52" and when packed the dimensions are 3.5" x 11". It weighs 1.1 lbs.

templates.name: How long will the Neapolitan Dough last in my freezer?
templates.content: Our Neapolitan Dough undergoes a fresh to flash-frozen process, helping to lock-in both freshness and an extended shelf life. We recommend using your dough within 4 months of receiving it.

templates.name: How big are the Juniper logs?
templates.content: The logs are pre-cut to 10-12" long and 3-6" wide, making them a perfect fit for your fire pit.

templates.name: Could I use Mini Oak Firewood for other Solo Stove products?
templates.content: Yes! Because of its size and low-moisture content, Cooking Wood is versatile enough for kindling and cooking in virtually all Solo Stove Fire Pits and Camp Stoves.

templates.name: Can I paint the colored fire pit another color?
templates.content: This ceramic coating is specifically designed for high-heat applications. You should not try to paint your own fire pit. Doing so will also void the warranty.

templates.name: Does Mini Oak Firewood add any flavor?
templates.content: Yes, you can expect a clean, earthy, wood-fired flavor.

templates.name: Are there preservatives in the Artisan set that help increase shelf life?
templates.content: There are none for our artisan dough, cheese, and sauce. These products are made fresh so that you can not only serve the freshest pizzas but also feel good about eating them. Our artisan Ezzo's pepperoni is made with Sodium Nitrate, a common shelf-life and flavor preservative used in a majority of processed meats.

templates.name: What are the allergens of the Neapolitan Dough?
templates.content: The Dough contains wheat and is made in a facility that handles eggs and soy.

templates.name: Is Solo Stove UL certified?
templates.content: All of Solo Stove's safety testing is done at our headquarters in Grapevine, Texas.

templates.name: Can the Grill be used as a fire-pit?
templates.content:
The Grill was designed to provide an optimal experience for backyard cooking and is not designed to function as a fire pit. The Grill doesn't allow air to come through the top of the unit. This prevents the food from being consumed by the secondary burn, thus it would not produce an efficient, smoke-free flame like the fire pits.

The Grill has a more shallow depth than our fire pits, providing less protection from a bonfire-sized flame. It's designed to support ideal cooking temperatures (approx. 400-500 degrees) which are much lower than the fire pits, which can sometimes reach over 1200 degrees Fahrenheit.

templates.name: How far away does the fire pit need to be away from a structure?
templates.content: We know that our fire pits can produce flames up to 4 feet high and have always recommended keeping at least 6 feet between your fire pit and any large structure - like your house, railings, or trees.

templates.name: Can I see the Nutrution Facts label for Neapolitan Dough?
templates.content: Yes, you can check it out here: https://prnt.sc/ReKRA5mAOdT1

templates.name: How tall is the Grill stand?
templates.content: The included Short Stand is 13 inches and creates a 21 inch cooking height when used with the Solo Stove Grill Body. The Tall Stand (sold separately) is 21.5 inches. This creates a cooking surface 28 inches off the ground. We recommend placing it on a hard, level surface.

templates.name: Does the fact that the stove is smokeless make it environmentally friendly?
templates.content:
The ingenious design of our Solo Stoves makes it easier to 'leave no trace' and to reduce your environmental impact. The completely contained ash pan keeps your burn waste and hot coals off the ground. The clean smokeless burn that draws you to the flame is also responsible for reducing the amount of volatiles released into the air. 

You can create the most positive impact by being mindful of your fuel, always taking care to use low-moisture hardwoods or eco-friendly composite logs.

templates.name: Can the grill also be a smoker?
templates.content: The grill is not designed to function as a smoker since the temperatures reach 400-500 degrees and stay there due to the convection function. It's optimized to grill for about 45 minutes with only 4 lbs of charcoal.

templates.name: Is solo stove environmentally certified?
templates.content: Solo Stove products are specifically designed for outdoor use, and EPA certifications for indoor appliance efficiency do not take fire pits or camp stoves into consideration.

templates.name: How do I wash my Rumpl?
templates.content: We recommend using a front loading washing machine on warm, and adding in Grangers brand detergent for the best cleaning results while preserving the DWR. Hang dry or toss in the dryer on tumble dry low.

templates.name: What are the ingredients for the Neapolitan Dough?
templates.content: The ingredients are "00" Caputo flour (Soft Wheat Flour Type "00"), water, salt, fresh yeast, and evaporated cane juice.

templates.name: How many uses until ashes need to be emptied in Mesa?
templates.content: Mesa should be emptied after every use. This will also ensure that you're getting the best airflow possible for each subsequent burn.

templates.name: What should you do when it starts to rain but the fire pit is burning hot?
templates.content: It's not safe to leave a fire unattended. Using sand to extinguish the flame is an option if you need to leave the fire. It's ideal to attend the fire until the flames have died down and it's just embers in the pit, then it's safe to add the Lid and head for cover.

templates.name: How long does charcoal last in the Grill?
templates.content: One small 4lb bag of charcoal keeps it at 400-500 degrees for up to 45 minutes solid. You can still cook burgers and steaks towards the end of that time!

templates.name: Will the Grill support a cast iron pan?
templates.content: Yes, you can cook with a cast iron pan on the cooking grate. Though the grill is designed to cook on the cooking grate, we don't foresee any issues with using a dutch oven directly on the coals at your own risk. Bear safety in mind when cooking directly on the coals.

templates.name: Is solo stove safe for people with allergies/COPD/asthma etc.?
templates.content: While we can't make any specific health claims, our customers find that the lower levels of smoke make it so that many can enjoy a fire who could not before. Those with medical concerns as it relates to being around a fire or smoke should seek medical advice before using.

templates.name: Can I leave Mesa outside, or should I bring it inside after use?
templates.content: Mesa is made of 304 stainless steel, so it will resist rusting for a time. However, it should be stored in a covered area when it is not being used. The good thing is that it's so small that it won't take up much space! One helpful tip is to store it inside your fire pit along with your other Solo Products.

templates.name: How many Recycled Firewood logs do I need at a time?
templates.content:
We recommend you start off with the following number of logs depending on the size of your fire pit:

- Ranger: 2-3 logs
- Bonfire: 3-4 logs
- Yukon: 4+ Logs

Add additional logs as needed to tend your fire about every 30 minutes.

templates.name: Are both sides of the blanket fire resistant?
templates.content: Only the green side of the blanket is made from fire-resistant fibers. The black side is made from recycled ripstop polyester.

templates.name: What if the color wipes off when I clean it?
templates.content: The color should not wipe off as long as you don't use any abrasive cleaners. Mild soap and water should be used to clean the pit and will not damage the surface.

templates.name: How much charcoal do you use to cook in your Grill?
templates.content: About 60 briquettes or 4lbs of charcoal will hold ideal cooking temperatures (approx. 400-500 degrees Fahrenheit) for up to 45 minutes.

templates.name: Student Discount
templates.content: You can learn more and apply for your discount here: https://connect.studentbeans.com/v4/hosted/solo-stove/us

templates.name: How do I start a return?
templates.content: You can start your return here: https://returns.narvar.com/solostove/returns?locale=en_US

templates.name: How long will thawed Neapolitan Dough last in the fridge?
templates.content: We recommend that thawed dough kept in an air-tight container in the fridge be used within days.

templates.name: What surfaces can I use Mesa on?
templates.content: Mesa was made as a tabletop fire pit, sized perfectly for any stable outdoor dining table to stylish end tables. Mesa should always be used with its included stand, especially when placed directly on wood, grass, PVC, or composite materials. Even while using the stand, it will still burn extremely hot and you should always be present while it's in use.

templates.name: Biggest difference between Solo Stove Grill and a Weber?
templates.content: Convection grilling! Our signature 360° Airflow technology creates the optimal convection cooking environment. Just put in a small amount of charcoal, light it, and the design naturally lets the air flow throughout to create the perfect cooking conditions. It uses less charcoal, heats up fast, and stays hot longer. That's why we say it's "Grilling, Reinvented."

templates.name: How long will my solo stove last?
templates.content: Your Solo Stove is sure to be the last one you ever own. It's built to last with you through the years and to share as many stories as an old friend.

templates.name: Can I refreeze the Neapolitan Dough after being fully thawed?
templates.content: No, please don't refreeze the dough once it has been fully thawed.

templates.name: How do I clean the exterior of the Mesa?
templates.content: To clean the colored versions, use a soft sponge or a damp microfiber towel, even a little bit of soap if you'd like. Note that the fire pit must be completely cool when cleaning it. For stainless, you can use Barkeepers Friend.

templates.name: Free shipping
templates.content: We offer free standard shipping on orders over $199 in the US (excluding Alaska and Hawaii). Maybe you can add something else to reach the minimum?

templates.name: Do you have gas inserts for the Mesa?
templates.content: We do not have gas inserts for our Mesa. We only recommend using wood or pellets for these units.

templates.name: Can I use my fire pit accessories with Surround?
templates.content: Absolutely! Surround was meant to enhance the safety of your fire pit but also enable you to use the accessories you already have. Enjoy the Fire Pit Cooking System, Shield and Roasting Sticks while sitting around the fire. If you use Deflector, only use the longer legs, and proceed with caution as it will raise the temperature of the tabletop surface on your Surround and in certain conditions the tabletop could get very hot.

templates.name: Where do I register my product?
templates.content: No registration is required to warranty your Solo Stove, because a better way is built-in. We want your Solo Stove to be the last one you ever own. It's built to last burn after burn, and our uncomplicated design provides a durable build that'll never fail. A lifetime warranty is at the heart of each Solo Stove, and the heart is the pursuit of good moments that can be shared with people who make your life better.

templates.name: Sales Collection
templates.content: You can take a look at our sale collection here: https://solostove.com/en-us/c/holiday-deals

templates.name: Can I get the Firepit + Surround bundle in a different color?
templates.content: While we are pleased to provide a variety of bundle options for our stainless steel fire pits, we don't offer colored fire pits for the Firepit + Surround bundle. The default option is Stainless steel and the Surround, but please note that the Surround is only offered in one color, which is black/dark gray. We apologize for any inconvenience or frustration this may have caused.
